tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57884,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Document FoundryEnrichmentProcessor span attributes and override behavior","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57877) by `@needuv` ## What Changed `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` was updated so that `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` are now stamped in `OnEnd` rather than `OnStart`. This ensures those tags take precedence over any values set by underlying frameworks (e.g. the OpenAI SDK) during the span's lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` continues to be set in `OnStart`. ## Gaps Found - The **Telemetry** section of the README mentions OpenTelemetry integration but does not describe `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` or the specific span attributes it sets. - The README does not document the environment variables that drive agent identity enrichment (`FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME`, `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION`, `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID`) in the context of telemetry. - The README does not explain the `gen_ai.agent.id` derivation (`agentName:agentVersion` when both are present, otherwise `agentName`). - The README does not explain the `OnEnd` override rationale — a key behavioral characteristic for users debugging unexpected span attribute values. - The CHANGELOG for the upcoming `1.0.0-beta.21` release does not mention this behavioral change (tag timing shifted from `OnStart` to `OnEnd`). 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Update CHANGELOG **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/CHANGELOG.md` **Action:** Edit — add a new entry in the `1.0.0-beta.21 (Unreleased)` `### Features Added` block. Add after the existing `FoundryEnvironment` bullet: ```markdown - `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` now stamps `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` in `OnEnd` instead of `OnStart`, ensuring they take precedence over any values set by underlying frameworks (e.g. the OpenAI SDK) during a span's lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` continues to be set in `OnStart`. ``` ### Step 2: Update README Telemetry section **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` **Action:** Edit — expand the existing `### Telemetry` subsection under `## Key concepts` to add the following content after the existing paragraph: ```markdown #### Foundry agent span enrichment `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` is an OpenTelemetry span processor that automatically enriches every span with Foundry agent identity attributes. It is registered automatically when you call `AddAgentHostTelemetry()` — no additional configuration is needed. The following attributes are set on **every** span: | Attribute | Source environment variable | Set in | |---|---|---| | `microsoft.foundry.project.id` | `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID` | `OnStart` | | `gen_ai.agent.name` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME` | `OnEnd` | | `gen_ai.agent.version` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION` | `OnEnd` | | `gen_ai.agent.id` | Derived: `{FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME}:{FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION}` (or `{FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME}` when version is absent) | `OnEnd` | `gen_ai.agent.*` attributes are set in `OnEnd` so that they override any values that an underlying framework (for example the OpenAI SDK) may have stamped during the span's lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` is set in `OnStart` because project membership is always known before any work begins. Only attributes whose corresponding environment variable is set are stamped; unset variables result in the attribute being omitted from the span. ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Start a server) 2. ✅ Key concepts (AgentHost, AgentHostBuilder, AgentHostApp, FoundryEnvironment, Telemetry, Health endpoint) 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing No structural changes are needed; only content additions to the **Telemetry** subsection. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no unexpected README changes 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected for this processor (it is `internal sealed`), but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24102623130/agentic_workflow) · ● 280.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57884","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-07T20:36:57Z","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:36:57Z"}

This ensures those tags take precedence over any values set by underlying frameworks (e.g. the OpenAI SDK) during the span's lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` continues to be set in `OnStart`. ## Gaps Found - The **Telemetry** section of the README mentions OpenTelemetry integration but does not describe `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` or the specific span attributes it sets. - The README does not document the environment variables that drive agent identity enrichment (`FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME`, `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION`, `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID`) in the context of telemetry. - The README does not explain the `gen_ai.agent.id` derivation (`agentName:agentVersion` when both are present, otherwise `agentName`). - The README does not explain the `OnEnd` override rationale — a key behavioral characteristic for users debugging unexpected span attribute values. - The CHANGELOG for the upcoming `1.0.0-beta.21` release does not mention this behavioral change (tag timing shifted from `OnStart` to `OnEnd`). 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Update CHANGELOG **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/CHANGELOG.md` **Action:** Edit — add a new entry in the `1.0.0-beta.21 (Unreleased)` `### Features Added` block. Add after the existing `FoundryEnvironment` bullet: ```markdown - `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` now stamps `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` in `OnEnd` instead of `OnStart`, ensuring they take precedence over any values set by underlying frameworks (e.g. the OpenAI SDK) during a span's lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` continues to be set in `OnStart`. ``` ### Step 2: Update README Telemetry section **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` **Action:** Edit — expand the existing `### Telemetry` subsection under `## Key concepts` to add the following content after the existing paragraph: ```markdown #### Foundry agent span enrichment `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` is an OpenTelemetry span processor that automatically enriches every span with Foundry agent identity attributes. It is registered automatically when you call `AddAgentHostTelemetry()` — no additional configuration is needed. The following attributes are set on **every** span: | Attribute | Source environment variable | Set in | |---|---|---| | `microsoft.foundry.project.id` | `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID` | `OnStart` | | `gen_ai.agent.name` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME` | `OnEnd` | | `gen_ai.agent.version` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION` | `OnEnd` | | `gen_ai.agent.id` | Derived: `{FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME}:{FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION}` (or `{FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME}` when version is absent) | `OnEnd` | `gen_ai.agent.*` attributes are set in `OnEnd` so that they override any values that an underlying framework (for example the OpenAI SDK) may have stamped during the span's lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` is set in `OnStart` because project membership is always known before any work begins. Only attributes whose corresponding environment variable is set are stamped; unset variables result in the attribute being omitted from the span. ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Start a server) 2. ✅ Key concepts (AgentHost, AgentHostBuilder, AgentHostApp, FoundryEnvironment, Telemetry, Health endpoint) 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing No structural changes are needed; only content additions to the **Telemetry** subsection. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no unexpected README changes 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected for this processor (it is `internal sealed`), but run to confirm 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24102623130/agentic_workflow) · ● 280.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57884","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-07T20:36:57Z","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:36:57Z"}

{"total_count":23,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4090517017,"number":57183,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization","body":"## Bug description
When using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.

The exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:
`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`

## Package and environment
- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`
- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`
- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents
- OS: Windows
- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`

## Expected behavior
After the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.

## Actual behavior
The full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.

## Reproduction notes
1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.
2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.
3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.
4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.

## Minimal observed output
```text
assistant(stream)> <full answer omitted>
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...
[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent
Unhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel<Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall>.Write(...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()
   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()
```

## Suspected root cause
The generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.

Relevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`

## Impact
- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.
- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.
- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.

## Workaround currently used
Application-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.

## Request
Please confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.","author_association":"MEMBER","user":{"login":"brandonh-msft","id":20270743,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjcwNzQz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20270743?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/brandonh-msft","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1069434707,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention","name":"Service Attention","color":"10066b","description":"Workflow: This issue is responsible by Azure service team.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDY5NDM0NzA3"},{"id":1178447860,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Client","name":"Client","color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a non-management package","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ3ODYw"},{"id":1353316183,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported","name":"customer-reported","color":"3800e0","description":"Issues Streaming update result in "The input does not contain any JSON tokens" exception","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8

### Describe the bug

Sometimes (not always) when I iterate over `AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate>` I get an error stating:

```
The input does not contain any JSON tokens. Expected the input to start with a valid JSON token, when isFinalBlock is true. LineNumber: 0 | BytePositionInLine: 0.
```

* I have the same problem with both `gpt-4.1` and `gpt-5`
* **Worth pointing out that it works if I use the same agent and prompt from the AI Foundry Agent  playground**

The error comes after `UpdateKind` = RunInProgress

```csharp
PersistentThreadMessage messageResponse = _client.Messages.CreateMessage(
    threadId: thread.Id,
    role: MessageRole.User,
    content: request.Prompt);

AsyncCollectionResult<StreamingUpdate> stream = _client.Runs.CreateRunStreamingAsync(
    threadId: thread.Id,
    agentId: _agent.Id
);

await foreach (StreamingUpdate streamingUpdate in stream) // This is where the error occurs after RunInProgress
{
      _logger.LogInformation(streamingUpdate.UpdateKind.ToString());
}
```

### Expected behavior

If this is due to a problem with the payload, such as an empty response from the LLM or a throttling error, I would expect to see this error

### Actual behavior

I assume the response from the LLM is not in an expected format and that this is an internal bug. 

### Reproduction Steps

N/A

### Environment

ChatClientAgent.RunAsync throws JSON parsing error with gpt-5 but works with gpt-4o","body":"### Library name and version

Microsoft.Agents.AI 1.0.0-preview.260128.1

### Describe the bug

We’re encountering a JSON parsing exception when using ChatClientAgent.RunAsync with the gpt-5 model. The same code works correctly when switching to gpt-4o.

This appears to be either a service-side incompatibility with Agents or an SDK parsing issue when handling the final response block.

### Expected behavior

ChatClientAgent.RunAsync returns an AgentResponse

### Actual behavior

ChatClientAgent.RunAsync throws an exception:

`The input does not contain any JSON tokens. Expected the input to start with a valid JSON token, when isFinalBlock is true.
LineNumber: 0 | BytePositionInLine: 0.
`

### Reproduction Steps

```    
    var chatMessage = new ChatMessage
    {
      Role = ChatRole.User,
      Contents = [new DataContent(image, "image/png")]
    };

    var response = await _chatClientAgent.RunAsync(chatMessage, session, cancellationToken=[REDACTED] Environment

Azure.AI.OpenAI.Assistants package - do you plan to deprecate it ?","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.OpenAI.Assistants

### Query/Question

Hi everyone, I'm a developer at DevExpress.  
We have functionality that allows [integrating assistants into the Blazor Chat](https://docs.devexpress.com/Blazor/DevExpress.AIIntegration.Blazor.Chat.DxAIChat#ai-service-assistants).

We also support [Azure Open AI Assistant use](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/foundry-models/concepts/models-sold-directly-by-azure?view=foundry-classic&tabs=global-standard-aoai%2Cglobal-standard&pivots=azure-openai#assistants-preview). Our solution is built using the <https://www.nuget.org/packages/OpenAI> nuget package.

However, OpenAI plans to deprecate the Assistants API on **August 26, 2026** — details here: <https://platform.openai.com/docs/assistants/migration>. They recommend migrating to the Responses API.
We are deciding where to migrate, and we want to understand what will happen to Azure AI Assistants in general. And what are the differences between Azure.AI.OpenAI.Assistants and Azure.AI.OpenAI.

I have a few questions regarding the older NuGet package <https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.OpenAI.Assistants/1.0.0-beta.4>.  
Am I correct in assuming that this package is deprecated and that you recommend using <https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.AI.OpenAI/2.8.0-beta.1> instead?
Will you deprecate Assistants in Azure?  And if so, will there be a guide on how to migrate existing assistants?


### Environment

- MCPToolDefinition does not support the project_connection_id property like in Python MCPTool class","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent 1.2.0-beta.8

### Describe the bug

The class MCPToolDefinition has no way to set the `project_connection_id` property like we can on the [MCPTool class](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-ai-agents/azure.ai.agents.models.mcptool?view=azure-python-preview) in python.

The `project_connection_id` is a property that is necessary when creating an agent that uses a MCP tool already created in Foundry nextgen.

Here are some examples in python:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/main/sdk/ai/azure-ai-projects/samples/agents/tools/sample_agent_mcp_with_project_connection.py#L48

```python
    tool = MCPTool(
        server_label="api-specs",
        server_url="https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp",
        require_approval="always",
        project_connection_id=os.environ["MCP_PROJECT_CONNECTION_ID"],
    )
```

I believe the `project_connection_id` is metadata, in that it does not need to be in the MCPToolResource (i.e. per thread run) thus why it could be in the MCPToolDefinition.

### Expected behavior

Be able to have extra dynamic parameters that can be passed to the REST API of Foundry to align with the Foundry nextgen platform.

### Actual behavior

Impossible to set the the property in any shape or form.

### Reproduction Steps

```csharp
new MCPToolDefinition("MCP-Server-Label", "MCP-Remote-URL")
```

MCPToolDefinition does not expose the `project_connection_id` property

### Environment

OS: Windows 11 25H2 26200.6584

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` around `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` — specifically the span attributes it sets, the environment variables that drive enrichment, the `gen_ai.agent.id` derivation logic, and the rationale for stamping `gen_ai.agent.*` attributes in `OnEnd` rather than `OnStart`. It also flags a missing CHANGELOG entry for the upcoming `1.0.0-beta.21` release.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (upcoming `1.0.0-beta.21`)
**Affected API:** `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` (OpenTelemetry span processor)
**Scenarios:**
- README Telemetry section does not describe `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` or its span attributes
- README does not document the environment variables (`FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME`, `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION`, `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID`) used for enrichment
- README does not explain `gen_ai.agent.id` derivation (`agentName:agentVersion` or `agentName`)
- README does not explain why `gen_ai.agent.*` are set in `OnEnd` vs `OnStart`
- CHANGELOG for `1.0.0-beta.21` is missing the behavioral change entry for tag-timing shift

**Root ask:** Expand the README's Telemetry subsection with a table of span attributes and their source env vars, explain the `OnEnd` override rationale, and add a CHANGELOG entry for the `OnStart`→`OnEnd` behavioral change.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue triggered by commit `b8540535` (PR #57877). No runtime bug is reported.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` implementation in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` to confirm the attribute table and `OnEnd`/`OnStart` behavior described in the issue.
2. Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` — add a "Foundry agent span enrichment" subsection under `### Telemetry` with the attribute table (attribute, source env var, set-in phase).
3. Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/CHANGELOG.md` — add a `1.0.0-beta.21` entry noting the `OnStart`→`OnEnd` timing change for `gen_ai.agent.*` attributes.
4. Validate with `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to confirm no unintended changes.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — the issue concerns an SDK client library (`Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`) in `/sdk/agentserver/`, not a management or provisioning package
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — the package lives under `/sdk/agentserver/`, which maps to the `%AI Agents` ServiceLabel in CODEOWNERS
- **Confidence:** High — the package name, service directory, and CODEOWNERS mapping are all unambiguous; no other service or category label was plausible
</details>
